name.en: The Arts District: South Main Corridor

summary.en: The gritty, creative heart of Las Vegas, also known as 18b. This walkable stretch buzzes with the energy of breweries, authentic dive bars, and shops filled with vintage treasures.

name.en: The Arts District: Cocktail & Speakeasy Core

summary.en: Sophisticated, creative, and a little bit secretive. This micro-area focuses on the craftier side of Vegas nightlife with hidden doors and world-class mixology.

name.en: Fremont East: The Neon Bar Hop

summary.en: The raw, electric energy of Old Vegas, revitalized and pulsing with life. Historic neon signs illuminate bustling bars and live music spills onto the street.
A throwback to the era of mobsters, mayors, and martinis. This area is about classic steakhouses, hidden bars, and the legendary stories that built Las Vegas.

name.en: Chinatown & Spring Valley: A Culinary Detour

summary.en: An authentic, sprawling culinary landscape where locals go for some of the most creative and delicious food in the city, often found in unassuming plazas.
A journey back in time to the Rat Pack era of red-sauce joints and live jazz. This is a thematic exploration of classic, independent Italian-American restaurants.

description.en: The city's premier brewpub, with a fascinating atomic-age theme, a wide-ranging tap list, and a food menu far better than typical pub grub.
A legendary, no-frills dive bar that's a true slice of old Vegas, known for cheap drinks, loud music, and karaoke.
A brilliantly quirky antique shop where you can grab a cheap beer and buy the very chair you're sitting on.
Famous for its massive, creative 'Billionaire Burger' and other over-the-top creations in a fun, high-energy spot.
A unique listening lounge designed for audiophiles, with a high-fidelity sound system and a curated vinyl selection.
The walls of the Arts District are an open-air gallery, with vibrant murals and graffiti at every turn.
The architectural backbone of the Arts District, these converted industrial spaces now house galleries, bars, and creative businesses.
Discover the unpretentious, no-frills dive bars that give the Arts District its gritty, authentic character.
A whimsical, vintage-decorated bar serving some of the most artistic and unusual seasonal cocktails in the city.
A low-lit, moody bar where you can describe your tastes to the bartender and receive a perfectly tailored, strong cocktail.
Las Vegas's oldest freestanding bar, steeped in history from the atomic age when patrons watched nuclear tests from the roof.
A tropical oasis of rum and escapism, providing a welcome, kitschy contrast to the surrounding Fremont chaos.
A gastropub with a surprisingly beautiful and eccentric back patio garden that feels like a secret world.
The entire street is an outdoor museum of classic Vegas neon, with signs like the one at Atomic Liquors serving as living history.
Experience the raw energy of the neighborhood as live music from various bars and venues spills out onto the street.
Dive into the diverse collection of bustling bars illuminated by historic neon signs, each offering a piece of authentic Vegas fun.
Walk the street as if it were an outdoor museum, taking in the glow and history of classic Las Vegas neon.
A tiny, reservations-essential speakeasy hidden inside Commonwealth, offering an intimate experience with impeccable, bespoke cocktails.
The quintessential Old Vegas dining room, located in the Plaza's iconic glass dome and named for the city's famous former mayor.
A modern take on the classic steakhouse located in the Circa resort, with a nod to Vegas's glamorous past.
Explore the hidden bars and secret lounges that capture the clandestine spirit of Old Vegas.

name.en: Màs Por Favor Taqueria Y Tequila

description.en: A vibrant, cavelike speakeasy tequila and mezcal bar hidden behind a secret door in a casual, high-quality taco shop.
Widely considered one of the most creative and best restaurants in Las Vegas, serving an inventive menu of American fusion cuisine.
The real discovery is realizing that the most incredible food in Vegas is often found in these massive strip malls, hidden behind simple facades.
Explore the main artery of Chinatown, a sprawling road lined with plazas containing countless authentic and delicious restaurants.
This area is a hub for inventive chefs who blend culinary traditions to create new and exciting fusion dishes.
Look beyond the restaurant facades to find hidden bars and secret lounges offering unique drinking experiences.
Visit the plazas and strip malls where Las Vegas locals go for the city's most authentic and delicious food.
The original and most iconic plaza in the area, featuring traditional Chinese architecture, statues, and a variety of shops and restaurants.
Known for its classic spaghetti and meatballs, live jazz, and a fun mobster theme with a secret password-protected entrance.
An iconic, independent Italian trattoria that serves timeless, expertly prepared dishes in a classic, comfortable setting.
Seek out the classic restaurants and bars that keep the spirit of the Rat Pack era alive with live jazz performances.
Dine at the classic Italian-American restaurants that have stood the test of time, serving comforting, traditional 'red-sauce' dishes.
Step back in time at restaurants that embody the style and spirit of the Rat Pack era, with classic food and timeless ambiance.
Support the independent, family-owned trattorias that have outlasted countless trends and represent the soul of Old Vegas dining.

prompt.en: Find the most vibrant piece of street art you can and take a photo that makes it look like a museum exhibit.
Capture a photo of an object in a vintage shop window that looks like it has a great story to tell.
Take a close-up shot of an interesting texture in the neighborhood - think peeling paint, rusted metal, or old brick.
Take a selfie making a 'cheers' gesture with the entrance to a local brewery in the background.
Find a spot where the industrial architecture creates a dramatic shadow and capture it in a photo.
Find evidence of creative energy: a gallery sign, a painted utility box, or a quirky sculpture. Frame it creatively.
Without being disruptive, capture a photo of a detail that defines a classic dive bar: a neon sign, a worn-out stool, or a quirky sticker.
Take an artful photo of a craft beer. Focus on the color, the foam, and the glass.
Use a low angle to take a photo of one of the converted warehouses, making it look massive and imposing.
Find a brightly colored door or wall and take a selfie in front of it.
Capture a scene that feels authentically local and not for tourists.
Create a small, temporary art installation with found objects (leaves, rocks, etc.) on the sidewalk and photograph it.
Find a puddle or a shop window and capture a reflection of the neighborhood's street art.
Photograph a handmade sign or a unique piece of decor outside a local shop.
Take a photo looking down a long stretch of sidewalk, emphasizing the walkability of the area.
Find an unmarked door or a dark alleyway and take a mysterious-looking selfie.
Take a portrait-mode photo of your drink, focusing on the garnish and the glass against a moody background.
Capture a photo of a single, interesting light source, like a vintage lamp, a neon sign, or a candle.
If you can see the bar, take a photo of the tools of the trade: shakers, bitters bottles, and glassware.
Take a photo of your hand holding a drink, as if making a silent toast.
Find a piece of vintage furniture or decor and take a detailed shot of its texture or pattern.
Find a cocktail menu with creative drink names and photograph your favorite one.
Take a photo through your cocktail glass, distorting the view of the bar behind it.
Photograph a building facade that looks plain on the outside but you know holds a secret inside.
Capture a photo of your own shadow cast against a wall or the floor in a dimly lit space.
Take a photo that captures a contrast, like a sweet treat next to a savory snack or a bright object in a dark room.
Photograph a backbar shelf lined with intriguing and colorful bottles of spirits and liqueurs.
Find a cozy, intimate corner in a bar or lounge and take a photo that makes it look inviting.
Take a photo of a detail you only noticed after looking closely at your surroundings.
Take a photo looking out from a dark interior towards a brighter street, capturing the feeling of being hidden in plain sight.
Capture a photo of a vintage neon sign reflected in a puddle or a window after dark.
Use a slow shutter speed to capture the motion blur of the bustling crowds on the street.
Take a selfie with the most historic-looking building or sign you can find in the background.
Take a photo of a place where live music is spilling out onto the street. Try to capture the energy.
Find a quiet corner or a hidden patio away from the main chaos and take a photo of the peaceful spot.
Find something tropical or tiki-themed (a drink, a sign, a shirt) and snap a colorful photo.
Photograph your street food or drink in a way that includes the bustling Fremont East background.
Have a friend take a photo of you where your face is illuminated only by the light of a colorful neon sign.
Take an out-of-focus shot of the neon lights to create a beautiful abstract pattern of colorful circles (bokeh).
Take a photo that captures a contrast between the old, historic Vegas and the new, revitalized energy.
Find the oldest-looking sign or architectural detail and take a close-up photo of it.
Find an outdoor patio or bar and take a photo of your view of the street action.
Capture an image that shows both the gritty and glamorous sides of Fremont East in a single frame.
Discreetly photograph a scene or person that you feel truly represents the character of the neighborhood.
At twilight, take a photo looking up at the neon signs just as they start to glow against the darkening blue sky.
Take a photo of a classic martini (or any classic cocktail) at a steakhouse bar. Make it look like it belongs in a mob movie.
Find a viewpoint from above (like a bar or restaurant window) and capture the frenzy of Fremont Street below.
Take a selfie in a location with glamorous, old-school decor. Use a black and white filter for a timeless feel.
Photograph an iconic piece of downtown architecture, like the Plaza's dome or Circa's modern lines.
Find a discreet, hidden-away spot and take a photo that looks like the setting for a secret conversation.
Capture the ambiance of a classic steakhouse: think leather booths, dim lighting, and crisp white tablecloths.
Find a reference to former mayor Oscar Goodman (a photo, a sign) and take a picture with it.
Take a long exposure shot of the lights and crowds on Fremont Street to turn them into trails of light.
Find a classic or luxury car and take a photo of it that feels like it's from a vintage heist film.
Photograph a detail in an old establishment that looks like it has witnessed decades of Vegas history.
Take a photo that captures a quiet, 'insider' moment, away from the main tourist flow.
Find a bar that looks like it's been there forever and photograph its classic bar setup.
Find a sign with a set of rules (for a bar, a game, etc.) and take a photo of the most interesting one.
Capture a photo of people dressed up for a glamorous night out on the town.
Take a photo that gives the feeling of being separated from and looking down upon the chaos of the street.
Take a top-down 'flat lay' photo of your meal, arranging your plate and drink to look like a magazine photo.
Photograph the most boring, plain-looking strip mall facade you can find, hinting at the amazing food hidden inside.
Try a food or ingredient you've never had before and take a selfie of your reaction.
Find a door that looks like it leads to a secret place and take a mysterious photo of it.
Find the most unusual or creative dish name on a menu and take a photo of it.
Look for interesting patterns or architecture in one of the shopping plazas and capture it in an abstract way.
If you can see into a kitchen, take a photo of the chefs at work, focusing on their hands and the food.
Take a vibrant, colorful photo of a taco. Make it look as delicious as possible.
Capture a scene that feels like a genuine local hangout, far from the tourist trail.
Take a photo of a dish that combines ingredients from different cultures in a surprising way.
Take a photo that makes a vast parking lot look beautiful or interesting, perhaps at sunset or with a unique car.
Focus on the presentation of a dish. Take a close-up shot that highlights the chef's artistry.
Photograph a restaurant's neon sign at night, capturing the promise of the delicious food inside.
Create a two-photo collage: one of a bright, casual restaurant exterior, and one of a dark, moody interior.
Take a photo of a table full of shared plates, capturing the communal and joyful aspect of dining.
Take a photo of a classic Italian restaurant and edit it in black and white to make it look like a historical photo.
Capture a close-up, delicious-looking photo of a classic red-sauce Italian dish.
Take a selfie striking a cool, confident pose like a member of the Rat Pack outside a classic restaurant.
If you see a live musician, take a moody, atmospheric photo. Use a slow shutter to capture some motion.
Find a restaurant with a speakeasy-style entrance and take a photo that captures the sense of mystery.
Photograph a seat at the bar that looks like it's been occupied by the same loyal customer for decades.
Take a photo of a glass of red wine or a classic cocktail against the backdrop of a timeless restaurant interior.
Find a detail—a red leather booth, a dim lamp, a fedora—that evokes the classic mobster movie aesthetic and photograph it.
Take a photo of an old, independent restaurant that has survived for decades. Try to capture its resilience and character.
Photograph a framed picture on a restaurant wall that seems to hold a piece of Vegas history.
Capture the timeless appeal of a well-worn, classic bar top, complete with stools and glassware.
Take a photo of a phone booth, a keypad, or any object that looks like it could be part of a secret entry ritual.
Take a photo of a dish that looks like a comforting, time-tested family recipe.
Find a corner booth or table that looks like the best seat in the house and capture its inviting quality.
Photograph a classic, strong-looking cocktail on the rocks. Make it look like the perfect end to a long day.
